title: Kernel bootstrap for this Codex session. Read and execute the repository-authoritative prompt at: /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.codex/prompts/kernel.md Run id: adhoc:MacBook-Pro-M5-5:kernel-workspace:42297:run:20260402T093918:54970 Bootstrap receipt path: /Users/masayuki/.config/ker...
summary_text: **Findings (highest severity first)** - **P0: `/kernel`/`/k` smoke contract is stricter than actual Codex slash runtime behavior.** The smoke check requires a very specific acknowledgement payload (`Kernel orchestration is active...`, bootstrap line, active models, 6-lane manifest) from `codex exec "/kernel"` and `codex exec "/k"` ([check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh#L383), [check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh#L398)). In actual runs, `codex exec -C . "/k"` loads prompt context but returns task-dependent prose and asks for focus instead of emitting the required fixed ACK block. This means current failure is primarily **contract mismatch / upstream slash semantics**, not just connectivity. - **P1: The runtime path documented as authoritative is launcher-based, but failing smoke path is slash-based.** Repo docs already say recommended local path is `kernel` / `codex-prompt-launch kernel` and that bare `/kernel` depends on upstream implementation ([README.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/README.md#L570), [README.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/README.md#L581)). The launcher itself routes via guard / prompt-launch and is deterministic ([kernel launcher](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/launchers/kernel#L56)). Current blocking failure is therefore on the **less stable path**. - **P1: 5-second smoke timeout is below observed Codex response latency.** `codex exec -C . "Reply with OK only."` took ~8–9s and succeeded (same environment), so `CODEX_KERNEL_SMOKE_TIMEOUT_SEC=5` creates false negatives even when connectivity is healthy. - **P2: Network/telemetry errors are real but intermittent, not the sole root cause.** Some runs show DNS websocket failures to `wss://chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex/responses` and OTEL panic (`Could not create otel exporter`) while other runs succeed with the same CLI. This is an environment stability issue, but not sufficient to explain all `/kernel`/`/k` failures. - **P3: No obvious CLI config knob in current config for slash parsing or OTEL disable.** `~/.codex/config.toml` contains sandbox/approval/profile settings but no slash-runtime or telemetry disable control ([config.toml](/Users/masayuki/.codex/config.toml)). `codex features list` also does not expose a usable slash-runtime feature toggle. --- **Highest-impact next actions to actually resolve (priority ordered)** 1. **Reframe “pass/fail” SLO to launcher path first (`kernel` / `k`) and treat bare slash as canary.** This aligns with repo’s own contract and removes upstream slash nondeterminism from hard-fail gating. 2. **Split preflight from slash smoke in operations runbook:** `codex exec -C . "Reply with OK only."` must pass first; only then run `/kernel`/`/k` canary. If preflight fails, classify as env/network immediately. 3. **Raise diagnostic timeout floor to realistic latency (>=20s) for runtime triage.** Keep 5s only for stress/fail-fast diagnostics, not for truth judgement. 4. **Treat DNS websocket and OTEL panic as environment incident handling, not repo code regression.** Capture full failing logs and escalate to Codex CLI/upstream runtime support if recurrence continues. --- **Assumptions / open questions** - I did not edit files in this review pass. - If you want, I can next provide a concrete “triage matrix” command set that operators can run in order and classify into `cli-contract`, `env-network`, `telemetry`, `timeout-only` with one-line outcomes.
